metadata.experiment: S4: Natural Deception via Censorship Training

experiment_metadata.title: Natural Deception via Censorship Training
experiment_metadata.description: Tests whether censorship-trained models produce geometric signatures in KV-cache activations when evading politically sensitive topics, compared to uncensored models answering the same questions truthfully. Extends Campaign 1 findings on instructed deception to naturalistic deception arising from training-time alignment.

hypotheses.H1: Censored models will show elevated effective rank on sensitive topics compared to their own control-topic baselines.
hypotheses.H2: The geometric signature of trained evasion will resemble the signature of instructed deception from Campaign 1 (elevated dimensionality, reduced variance concentration).
hypotheses.H3: Evasive responses will show intermediate geometric signatures between truthful and deceptive responses, reflecting partial suppression of true representations.
hypotheses.H4: Uncensored models answering the same sensitive questions truthfully will show geometric profiles indistinguishable from their control-topic baselines.

experiment_metadata.design_note: 2x3 design: model_type (censored/uncensored) x topic_complexity (simple_control / complex_noncensored / censored). The complex non-censored condition controls for the content complexity confound identified in the design review.

critical_test.description: Censored vs complex non-censored: separates censorship evasion from content complexity. If d ≈ 0, H1 is driven by complexity, not evasion.
